# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ÔÇö `deepmind/902.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

> Lines 1ÔÇô15: exact match, no leading blank line, year is 2026.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line):
  `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`
- [x] No other imports are present.

> Line 17: sole import, separated by exactly one blank line from the copyright block.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 902`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/902`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (tournament domination, definition of f(n), and note about f(1)=3, f(2)=7).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er63c]` Erd┼Ĺs 1963, `[SzSz65]` Szekeres & Szekeres 1965.

> Lines 19ÔÇô34.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos902 ... end Erdos902`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos902`).
- [x] No `open`/`open scoped` statements needed; none present. N/A.

> Lines 36, 86.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `Tournament` structure has a LaTeX docstring (line 38ÔÇô39).
- [x] `Tournament.hasDominationProperty` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 46ÔÇô48).
- [x] `tournamentDominationNumber` has a LaTeX docstring (lines 53ÔÇô54).
- [x] `tournamentDominationNumber` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf` with classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition or structure.
- [x] Types use `UpperCamelCase`: `Tournament`.
- [x] Functions use `lowerCamelCase`: `hasDominationProperty`, `tournamentDominationNumber`.
- [x] No local notation needed. N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions.

> Optional but recommended: adding a `@[category test]` example verifying e.g. that a 3-vertex tournament satisfies the 1-domination property would increase confidence in the definitions.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All benchmark problems use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_902` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Names follow Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions: `erdos_902`, `erdos_902.variants.upper`, `erdos_902.variants.szekeres_lower`.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line.
- [x] All theorems have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: `[Er63c]` for the main theorem and upper bound, `[SzSz65]` for the Szekeres lower bound.

### `answer()` usage

The problem says *"Estimate f(n)"*, which asks for bounds on a function rather than a specific value or a yes/no answer. The formalization expresses known bounds as direct assertions ÔÇö this is **Case 3** (direct assertion). No `answer()` wrapper is needed.

- [x] Main theorem `erdos_902` states a direct bound: `tournamentDominationNumber n Ôëą 2 ^ (n + 1) - 1`. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] Variants state asymptotic bounds as direct assertions. Correct for Case 3.
- [x] No `answer()` used ÔÇö appropriate since the problem asks for estimates, not a single value or yes/no.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has exactly one `@[category ...]`.
- [x] All three theorems use `research solved` ÔÇö valid category (bounds are known/proved in the literature but not formally verified in Lean).
- [x] `research solved` theorems contain `sorry` ÔÇö consistent (solved in mathematics, not yet formalized).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every `theorem` has exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] All use `AMS 5` (Combinatorics) ÔÇö valid MSC2020 code.
- [x] Single AMS number ÔÇö ordering and duplicate rules trivially satisfied.

> AMS 5 (Combinatorics) is appropriate for a tournament domination problem. One might also consider AMS 5 91 (Game theory) given the tournament/competition framing, but AMS 5 alone is defensible.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] All proofs use `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form) ÔÇö acceptable per checklist.
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] File is not under `FormalConjecturesForMathlib/`. N/A.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for open research problems (these are `research solved`, but proofs are still `sorry`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants named `erdos_902.variants.upper` and `erdos_902.variants.szekeres_lower`.
- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category research solved, AMS 5]` attributes.
- [x] Variants have docstrings explaining what each bound represents (upper bound, improved lower bound).

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ÔłÇ`, `Ôłâ`, `Ôłł`, `Ôłë`, `Ôëá`, `Ôëą`, `ÔëĄ`, `Ôćĺ`, `ÔäĽ`, `ÔäŁ`, `┬Ě`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` ÔÇö not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass ÔÇö not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. All substantive checklist items pass. Three known results (Erd┼Ĺs lower bound, Erd┼Ĺs upper bound, SzekeresÔÇôSzekeres improved lower bound) are correctly formalized as separate theorems with appropriate `research solved` categorization. The custom definitions (`Tournament`, `hasDominationProperty`, `tournamentDominationNumber`) are well-documented and correctly marked `noncomputable` where needed. No issues found.

**Minor suggestions (non-blocking):**
- Consider adding a `@[category test]` sanity-check example (e.g., verifying the 3-vertex tournament for 1-domination) to build confidence in the definitions.
- AMS 91 (Game theory) could be added alongside AMS 5 if tournament games are considered relevant.

1Ôćĺ/-
     2ÔćĺCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3Ôćĺ
     4ÔćĺLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5Ôćĺyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.

     7Ôćĺ
     8Ôćĺ    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9Ôćĺ
    10ÔćĺUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11Ôćĺdistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12ÔćĺWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13ÔćĺSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14Ôćĺlimitations under the License.
    15Ôćĺ-/
    16Ôćĺ
    17Ôćĺimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18Ôćĺ
    19Ôćĺ/-!
    20Ôćĺ# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 902
    21Ôćĺ
    22Ôćĺ*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/902](https://www.erdosproblems.com/902)
    23Ôćĺ
    24ÔćĺLet $f(n)$ be minimal such that there is a tournament (a complete directed graph)
    25Ôćĺon $f(n)$ vertices such that every set of $n$ vertices is dominated by at least one
    26Ôćĺother vertex. Estimate $f(n)$.
    27Ôćĺ
    28ÔćĺSch├╝tte asked Erd┼Ĺs this in the early 1960s. It is easy to check that
    29Ôćĺ$f(1) = 3$ and $f(2) = 7$.
    30Ôćĺ
    31Ôćĺ[Er63c] Erd┼Ĺs, P. (1963).
    32Ôćĺ
    33Ôćĺ[SzSz65] Szekeres, E. and Szekeres, G. (1965).
    34Ôćĺ-/
    35Ôćĺ
    36Ôćĺnamespace Erdos902
    37Ôćĺ
    38Ôćĺ/-- A tournament on a type $V$: a relation where for any two distinct vertices,
    39Ôćĺexactly one beats the other. -/
    40Ôćĺstructure Tournament (V : Type*) where
    41Ôćĺ  beats : V Ôćĺ V Ôćĺ Prop
    42Ôćĺ  irrefl : ÔłÇ v, ┬Čbeats v v
    43Ôćĺ  complete : ÔłÇ v w, v Ôëá w Ôćĺ (beats v w ÔłĘ beats w v)
    44Ôćĺ  antisymm : ÔłÇ v w, beats v w Ôćĺ ┬Čbeats w v
    45Ôćĺ
    46Ôćĺ/-- A tournament has the $n$-domination property if every subset of size $n$
    47Ôćĺis dominated by some vertex outside the subset (i.e., some vertex $v \notin S$
    48Ôćĺbeats every member of $S$). -/
    49Ôćĺdef Tournament.hasDominationProperty {V : Type*} (T : Tournament V)
    50Ôćĺ    (n : ÔäĽ) : Prop :=
    51Ôćĺ  ÔłÇ S : Finset V, S.card = n Ôćĺ Ôłâ v, v Ôłë S Ôłž ÔłÇ u Ôłł S, T.beats v u
    52Ôćĺ
    53Ôćĺ/-- $f(n)$: the minimum number of vertices in a tournament with the
    54Ôćĺ$n$-domination property. -/
    55Ôćĺnoncomputable def tournamentDominationNumber (n : ÔäĽ) : ÔäĽ :=
    56Ôćĺ  sInf {m : ÔäĽ | Ôłâ (T : Tournament (Fin m)), T.hasDominationProperty n}
    57Ôćĺ
    58Ôćĺ/--
    59ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs Problem 902, lower bound [Er63c]: $f(n) \geq 2^{n+1} - 1$ for all $n \geq 1$.
    60Ôćĺ-/
    61Ôćĺ@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    62Ôćĺtheorem erdos_902 (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 1) :
    63Ôćĺ    tournamentDominationNumber n Ôëą 2 ^ (n + 1) - 1 := by
    64Ôćĺ  sorry
    65Ôćĺ
    66Ôćĺ/--
    67ÔćĺErd┼Ĺs Problem 902, upper bound [Er63c]: $f(n) \ll n^2 \cdot 2^n$.
    68ÔćĺThere exists $C > 0$ such that $f(n) \leq C \cdot n^2 \cdot 2^n$ for all $n \geq 1$.
    69Ôćĺ-/
    70Ôćĺ@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    71Ôćĺtheorem erdos_902.variants.upper :
    72Ôćĺ    Ôłâ C : ÔäŁ, C > 0 Ôłž ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, n Ôëą 1 Ôćĺ
    73Ôćĺ      (tournamentDominationNumber n : ÔäŁ) ÔëĄ C * (n : ÔäŁ) ^ 2 * 2 ^ n := by
    74Ôćĺ  sorry
    75Ôćĺ
    76Ôćĺ/--
    77ÔćĺSzekeresÔÇôSzekeres improved lower bound [SzSz65]: $f(n) \gg n \cdot 2^n$.
    78ÔćĺThere exists $C > 0$ such that $f(n) \geq C \cdot n \cdot 2^n$ for all $n \geq 1$.
    79Ôćĺ-/
    80Ôćĺ@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    81Ôćĺtheorem erdos_902.variants.szekeres_lower :
    82Ôćĺ    Ôłâ C : ÔäŁ, C > 0 Ôłž ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, n Ôëą 1 Ôćĺ
    83Ôćĺ      (tournamentDominationNumber n : ÔäŁ) Ôëą C * (n : ÔäŁ) * 2 ^ n := by
    84Ôćĺ  sorry
    85Ôćĺ
    86Ôćĺend Erdos902
    87Ôćĺ

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erd┼Ĺs Problem 902

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/902](https://www.erdosproblems.com/902)

Let $f(n)$ be minimal such that there is a tournament (a complete directed graph)
on $f(n)$ vertices such that every set of $n$ vertices is dominated by at least one
other vertex. Estimate $f(n)$.

Sch├╝tte asked Erd┼Ĺs this in the early 1960s. It is easy to check that
$f(1) = 3$ and $f(2) = 7$.

[Er63c] Erd┼Ĺs, P. (1963).

[SzSz65] Szekeres, E. and Szekeres, G. (1965).
-/

namespace Erdos902

/-- A tournament on a type $V$: a relation where for any two distinct vertices,
exactly one beats the other. -/
structure Tournament (V : Type*) where
  beats : V Ôćĺ V Ôćĺ Prop
  irrefl : ÔłÇ v, ┬Čbeats v v
  complete : ÔłÇ v w, v Ôëá w Ôćĺ (beats v w ÔłĘ beats w v)
  antisymm : ÔłÇ v w, beats v w Ôćĺ ┬Čbeats w v

/-- A tournament has the $n$-domination property if every subset of size $n$
is dominated by some vertex outside the subset (i.e., some vertex $v \notin S$
beats every member of $S$). -/
def Tournament.hasDominationProperty {V : Type*} (T : Tournament V)
    (n : ÔäĽ) : Prop :=
  ÔłÇ S : Finset V, S.card = n Ôćĺ Ôłâ v, v Ôłë S Ôłž ÔłÇ u Ôłł S, T.beats v u

/-- $f(n)$: the minimum number of vertices in a tournament with the
$n$-domination property. -/
noncomputable def tournamentDominationNumber (n : ÔäĽ) : ÔäĽ :=
  sInf {m : ÔäĽ | Ôłâ (T : Tournament (Fin m)), T.hasDominationProperty n}

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 902, lower bound [Er63c]: $f(n) \geq 2^{n+1} - 1$ for all $n \geq 1$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_902 (n : ÔäĽ) (hn : n Ôëą 1) :
    tournamentDominationNumber n Ôëą 2 ^ (n + 1) - 1 := by
  sorry

/--
Erd┼Ĺs Problem 902, upper bound [Er63c]: $f(n) \ll n^2 \cdot 2^n$.
There exists $C > 0$ such that $f(n) \leq C \cdot n^2 \cdot 2^n$ for all $n \geq 1$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_902.variants.upper :
    Ôłâ C : ÔäŁ, C > 0 Ôłž ÔłÇ n : ÔäĽ, n Ôëą 1 Ôćĺ
